stories.title: D11 Lockdown Lifted at Twain Elementary — Police Investigated Non-Credible Threat [TIER 1]

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: UPDATE: Twain Elementary (D11) was placed on lockdown Wednesday after a threat was reported. The lockdown was lifted — police say there's nothing to indicate the threat was credible. All clear. 🏫 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: What happens during a D11 school lockdown? Police investigate, staff follow protocol, kids are kept safe and away from windows. Twain Elementary's quick all-clear yesterday is how these procedures are supposed to work. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
If you have kids at Twain Elementary in D11, here's what you need to know about yesterday.

On Wednesday, April 1, Twain Elementary School in Colorado Springs was placed on lockdown after a threat was reported. Police responded and investigated. Before noon, the lockdown was lifted. KKTV, the Gazette, and KOAA News5 all reported the same update: police say there's "nothing to indicate" the threat was credible. The school returned to normal operations.

The phrase "non-credible threat" means investigators determined the threat did not pose an actual danger. School lockdowns — even ones that end without incident — are taken seriously by D11 and Colorado Springs police. Students are moved to safe positions, communication goes out to staff, and police respond quickly.

For parents who got an alert yesterday and felt their anxiety spike: that's a completely normal response. The system worked the way it's supposed to work — the lockdown was precautionary, the threat was investigated, and kids were safe.

If you're a D11 parent and want to review the district's emergency response protocols or sign up for district alerts, visit d11.org or call your school's main office.

How do you handle the anxiety when your child's school goes on lockdown — even when it ends with all clear?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 8:30 AM MT

```
D11 Twain Elementary lockdown lifted April 1. Police say threat was non-credible. All clear.
```

article.headline: D11 Twain Elementary Lockdown Lifted: What Colorado Springs Parents Need to Know

stories.title: Pool Safety Alert — CPSC Recalls Evajoy & Blue Wave Above-Ground Pools Before Spring Season [TIER 1]

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Before spring pool setup: CPSC recalled Evajoy and Blue Wave pools (48"+ tall). A compression strap creates a foothold for kids to climb in — even with the ladder removed. Free repair: 888-846-2988. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Evajoy pool recall: ~4,000 units recalled. Models EJ-HF032, EJ-HF044, EJ-HF045. Ages 22 months–3 yrs most at risk. Spring is here — check your pool model NOW before set-up. cpsc.gov or 888-846-2988. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Colorado Springs families: before you set up your backyard pool this spring, there's a recall you need to know about.

The Consumer Product Safety Commission recalled two brands of above-ground pools — Evajoy and Blue Wave — for a serious drowning hazard. Here's the specific issue: these pools have an external compression strap that creates a foothold on the outside of the pool wall. That foothold allows young children to climb into the pool even after you've removed the ladder. The ladder, it turns out, isn't the only way in.

The CPSC links similar pool designs to 9 children's drowning deaths between 2007 and 2022. The victims ranged from 22 months to 3 years old.

For Evajoy specifically: about 4,000 pools were sold on Amazon between March 2023 and June 2024 at prices between $550 and $1,050. The recalled models are EJ-HF032 (16 ft × 48 in), EJ-HF044 (18 ft × 52 in), and EJ-HF045 (24 ft × 52 in).

What you should do:
- Stop using the pool immediately if you have one of these models
- Call Evajoy at 888-846-2988 (Monday–Friday, 9 a.m.–5 p.m. PT) for a free repair
- Or email support@evajoy.co

April is when COS families start thinking about pool season. Check your model before the first splash.

Do you always check if your kids' outdoor gear is on the CPSC recall list before using it each spring?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 10:30 AM MT

```
Pool recall alert: Evajoy and Blue Wave above-ground pools recalled due to drowning hazard. Check your model before spring setup. 888-846-2988.
```

article.headline: Pool Safety Alert: Evajoy and Blue Wave Above-Ground Pools Recalled Before Spring Setup Season

stories.title: US Measles Outbreak Approaches 1,300 Cases — COS Families Need an Updated MMR Check [TIER 2]

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: US measles cases are approaching 1,300 — and America's "eliminated" status is officially under review. If your kids are behind on MMR vaccines, call your pediatrician this week. Don't wait. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
The US measles outbreak has hit a number that's getting serious: nearly 1,300 confirmed cases, according to CIDRAP. And now, the American Academy of Pediatrics reports that the country's "measles-eliminated" status — which the US has held since 2000 — is officially under review.

This matters for COS families because measles is extraordinarily contagious. It spreads faster than COVID-19, it can cause serious complications in young children, and it's entirely preventable with the MMR vaccine.

The MMR vaccine (measles-mumps-rubella) is recommended by the AAP at two doses: one at 12–15 months, one at 4–6 years. Most COS kids are on schedule. But if there's any uncertainty — maybe you've moved recently, switched pediatricians, or your child missed a checkup — now is the right time to verify.

Also this season: 44 pediatric flu deaths have been reported nationally. Spring is not a time to let your guard down on preventable illness.

This is separate from the broader AAP vs. CDC vaccine schedule conversation we covered last week. The MMR vaccine and its schedule are consistent between both organizations — it's one of the things they agree on.

When did your family last update your kids' vaccine records? Is there anything you're not sure about?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 3:30 PM MT

```
US measles cases near 1,300. Elimination status under review. Check your kids' MMR status — call your COS pediatrician.
```

stories.title: CPSC Recalls Pull-String Teething Toys — Choking and Respiratory Hazard [TIER 2]

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Heading into Easter weekend: CPSC recalled AiTuiTui and Yetonamr pull-string teething toys. The silicone strings can lodge in an infant's throat — choking and respiratory hazard. Stop use immediately if you have these. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Easter weekend is three days away. If you're putting together baskets or picking up last-minute gifts for a baby or toddler, here's something to check before you buy.

Two brands of pull-string teething toys have been recalled by the CPSC: AiTuiTui (sold by "Vanfun" on Amazon) and Yetonamr. Both products have the same problem — the silicone strings can reach the back of an infant's throat and lodge there. The result can be respiratory distress, choking, or death.

The Yetonamr product also has silicone strings that are smaller than what the mandatory standard permits — a separate but related issue.

If you have either product at home, stop use immediately. If you were considering buying a pull-string teething toy on Amazon for a baby this weekend, do a quick CPSC search first (cpsc.gov/recalls).

The safest Easter basket move for infants: hard silicone teethers with no detachable parts, textured ring teethers, or frozen teething toys designed for little mouths. The ones with strings attached are worth a second look.

Do you keep up with CPSC recalls, or do you find them hard to track? We post these every week so you don't have to search.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 12:30 PM MT

```
Teething toy recall alert: AiTuiTui and Yetonamr pull-string teethers recalled — choking hazard. Stop use immediately. Check Amazon orders.
```

article.headline: CPSC Recalls Pull-String Teething Toys: AiTuiTui and Yetonamr Products Pose Choking and Respiratory Hazard

stories.title: Academy District 20 — 17 Years of "Accredited with Distinction" [TIER 3]

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: D20 has been "Accredited with Distinction" by Colorado for 17 years in a row. 26,000+ students, 40 schools, some of the state's highest grad rates. COS D20 families — your district is doing something right. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
We've shared a lot of heavy local education news this week — D11 lockdown, ongoing D49 budget challenges. So let's end Thursday with something that's worth saying out loud.

Academy District 20 has been "Accredited with Distinction" by the state of Colorado for 17 consecutive years. That's one of the longest streaks in the state.

D20 serves more than 26,000 students across 40 schools in Colorado Springs. It's recognized as having some of the highest graduation rates in the state. It's the kind of track record that's easy to take for granted when you're living in it — 17 years means a generation of kids has gone through this district with that distinction in place.

The district isn't perfect — no district is. But consistent academic accreditation at the highest level, year after year, means something real for the families in northern Colorado Springs who call D20 home.

If you're a D20 parent, what do you think makes the district special? And for families looking at COS school options — is accreditation a factor you consider?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 8:30 AM MT

```
Academy District 20: 17 years of "Accredited with Distinction." COS families in D20 — your district has earned it.
```
